# MAIN ARTICLE # PAPER Cite this: DOI: 10.1039/d0nj02387f Received11th May2020, Accepted7th July2020 DOI: 10.1039/d0nj02387f rsc.li/njc # Introduction Nanozymes are nanomaterials with enzyme-like activities. 1-3They were first reported by Yan et al. (2007) who produced Fe3O4nanoparticles which intrinsically promote peroxidaselike activity. 4Compared with natural enzymes, nanozymes possess excellent advantages of high-stability, low-cost, adjustable catalytic activity, and high tolerance ability in harsh environments, which have been wildly applied in the fields of biosensing, biotherapy, and environmental protection. 5Recently, many other nanozymes such as carbon, 6,7noble metal, 8-10and transition metal nanomaterials11,12have been described. Among them, MnO2nanoparticles with intrinsic oxidase activity have been proved, and thus successfully applied a Department of Medical Imaging, Henan Provincial People's Hospital& People's Hospital of Zhengzhou University, Zhengzhou, Henan, 450003, China. E-mail: mywang@ha.edu.cn b Henan Key Laboratory for Medical Imaging of Neurological Diseases, Henan Provincial People's Hospital & People's Hospital of Zhengzhou University, Zhengzhou, Henan, 450003, China c School of Clinical Medicine, Henan University, Zhengzhou, Henan, 450003, China d Gordon Center for Medical Imaging, Radiology, Massachusetts General Hospital, Harvard Medical School, 125Nashua Street, Boston, MA, 02114, USA. E-mail: ELFAKHRI.GEORGES@MGH.HARVARD.EDU † Electronic supplementary information (ESI) available. See DOI: 10.1039/ d0nj02387f View Article Online View Journal # Colorimetric biosensing of glucose in human serum based on the intrinsic oxidase activity of hollow MnO2nanoparticles † Lijuan Chen, abc Haiyan Gao, abc Yan Bai, abc Wei Wei, abc Junfeng Wang, d Georges El Fakhri* d and Meiyun Wang * abc Colorimetric analysis is a method for determining the concentration of a chemical compound in a solution using a color reagent. Here, we developed a low cost and rapid approach for cascade biosensing of glucose in human serum samples by taking into account the advantages of the excellent selectivity of natural glucose oxidase and the splendid catalytic properties of nanozymes. Hollow manganese dioxide nanoparticles (H-MnO2NPs) assembled by abundant nanosheets were successfully synthesized via a one-pot reduction strategy, demonstrating exhibited splendid oxidase-like activities. In the colorimetric analysis system, glucose oxidase can oxidize glucose to produce H2O2, which can consume the proposed H-MnO2NPs in acid medium. Then, the residual H-MnO2NPs can catalyze the3,3 0 ,5,5 0 -tetramethylbenzidine molecules and then produce a colorimetric signal at652nm, which can quantificationally reflect the concentrations of glucose. The lower limit of detection of glucose is0.84m M and the system has a linear response up to200m M. This method provides suitable specificity and practical feasibility to be used for the routine analysis of human serum samples. in both biosensing and biotherapy thanks to the tunable structures/morphologies, unique physical/chemical properties and excellent biosecurity. 13-15For example, Dong et al. (2017) developed a ratiometric fluorescent biosensor for the selective, ultrasensitive determination of glutathione concentration based on the oxidase-like properties of MnO2nanosheets. 16In addition, Chen et al. (2019), by using a sonochemical technique, showed that the same MnO2nanosheets could be developed with intrinsic glucose oxidase properties. 17 Specifically, using MnO2nanostructures for the detection of glucose had attracted great attention recently, demonstrating satisfactory feasibility in practical sample analysis, which played a crucial role in the management of chronic metabolic diseases such as diabetes mellitus and hypoglycemia. 18,19Based on diversified MnO2nanostructures, many advanced techniques including electrochemistry, 20colorimetry, 19fluorescence, 21and chemiluminescence, 22sensitive and selective detection of glucose based on the MnO2-involoved biosensing platforms are widely studied and available. Owing to its simplicity ( i.e. rapid visual display), practicality ( i.e. low demand for sophisticated instrumentation or expert skill) and low cost, colorimetric biosensing is one of the most wildly used approaches. 23The goal of the present study is to further enhance colorimetry based determination of glucose concentration by using nanostructures to provide ultra-sensitivity. In addition, it is well demonstrated that the nanozymes with Fig. 1Schematic diagram of cascade detection of glucose progress with hollow feature H-MnO2NPs. advanced structures always have excellent catalytic activity due to their large specific surface area, many exposed active sites, and unique chemical properties, 24,25which could improve the sensitivity of colorimetric biosensing platform. Compared with bulk MnO2nanoparticles, hollow MnO2nanoparticles not only possess a higher surface to volume ratio and larger pore volume, but also have much more catalytic/adsorption sites for substrates, 26demonstrating higher catalytic activity for the improvement of the sensitivity of colorimetric biosensing. Although some breakthroughs had been achieved recently, the rapid and simple preparation of hollow MnO2nanoparticles still faced huge challenges. We aimed to exploit the simple synthesis method of MnO2nanozymes with the hollow structure, and further provide robust and ultrasensitive colorimetric determination of glucose concentration in human serum samples. In our work, we proposed to maximize efficiency by using a so-called one-pot strategy whereby the reactant is subjected to successive chemical reactions in just one reactor. First, hollow MnO2nanoparticles (H-MnO2NPs) were rapidly prepared via mild in situ reduction of KMnO4by citric acid in the presence of traces of hydrochloric acid. The as-prepared H-MnO2NPs exhibited splendid oxidase activities owing to the abundant nanosheets assembled on the surface, which could provide more active sites to improve the catalytic properties. Accordingly, we design a colorimetric biosensor for glucose detection based on the natural glucose oxidase/nanozyme cascade catalytic reaction. Glucose oxidase (GOx) can oxidize glucose to produce H2O2which can consume H-MnO2NPs under acid conditions to generate Mn2+ . The residual H-MnO2NPs can further oxidize3,3 0 ,5,5 0 -tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to produce a colorimetric signal at652nm, which can quantificationally reflect the concentrations of glucose (Fig. 1). The high specificity and sensitivity of the colorimetric method for measuring glucose concentration demonstrated the practical potential in studies of human serum samples taken from patients with diabetes mellitus and controls. # Experimental section # Materials and instruments After obtaining approval from the Local Research Ethics Committee (REC), blood and serum samples were obtained at the Henan Provincial People's Hospital (China) from7patients with diabetes mellitus and7healthy control subjects. Prior to analysis, the samples were kept at /C0 20 1C. The following reagents and chemicals were used in the colorimetric method for measuring the concentration of glucose in the above samples. Potassium permanganate (KMnO4) (Yantai Shuangshuang Chemical Co. Ltd, China), 3,3 0 ,5,5 0 -tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) (Shanghai Macklin Biochemical Co. Ltd, China), glucose oxidase (Shanghai Aladdin Bio-Chem Technology Co. Ltd, China), acetic acid, glucose, disodium hydrogen phosphate dodecahydrate, sodium phosphate monobasic dihydrate, and ethanol (all from Tianjin Zhiyuan reagent Co. Ltd, China), citric acid monohydrate (Tianjin Hengxing Chemical Preparation Co. Ltd, China), hydrochloric acid (Luoyang Chemical Reagent Factory, Luoyang, China) and water deionized by a water purification system (Millipore, USA). Relevant instrumentation comprised a Quanta250FEG Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) (FEI, USA), to investigate the microstructure, and a JEM210Transmission Electron Microscope (TEM) (JEOL Ltd, Japan) equipped with energydispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) to measure the compositional distribution of the nanoparticles. X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS) spectra were obtained by using a XSAM800multifunctional surface analysis electron spectrometer (Kratos, UK) and Fourier transformed infrared spectroscopy (FTIR) spectra were obtained by using a Nicolet iS5 (Thermo Scientific, USA). X-ray Diffraction (XRD) was measured between10 1and80 1by using a diffractometer (D8Advance, Germany) and UV-vis spectra and absorbance were recorded by using a multimode microplate reader (Synergy H1, Bio Tek). All experiments were performed in accordance with the Helsinki Declaration, and approved by the ethics committee at Henan Provincial People's Hospital. Study participants were fully informed regarding the purposes of the study and consent was obtained. # Preparation of hollow MnO2nanoparticles (H-MnO2NPs) To produce H-MnO2NPs, 240mg (1.5mmol) of potassium permanganate (KMnO4) was dissolved in80mL of hydrochloric acid (HCl) solution (0.1mol) and stirred for2minutes. Next168mg (0.01mol) of citric acid was added to the mixture which was stirred for further20minutes. The solution turned to an earthy yellow colour and was centrifuged (2000rpm) for20minutes and the precipitate collected, purified by using deionized water and lyophilized. # Measurement of the intrinsic oxidase activity of H-MnO2NPs A volume of10m L of the H-MnO2NPs (100m g mL /C0 1 ) was added to100m L of HAC-NaAC buffer solution (pH = 4.0) and100m L TMB solution (1mM) was gradually added over a period of90seconds. The absorbance value and UV-vis spectra of the resulting solution were measured by using a multi-label microplate reader (Synergy H1, Bio Tek). Similar procedures were used to measure pH and to optimize incubation time, as well as to compare the catalytic properties of the H-MnO2NPs with that of other metal irons. The kinetic data were calculated by a typical Michaelis-Menten curve as to v = Vmax[S]/( Km + [S]), where v is the initial velocity, [S] is the concentration of the substrate, Km is the Michaelis-Menten constant, and Vmax is the maximal reaction velocity. # Determination of glucose concentration In separate trails, various concentrations of glucose from1to200m M were incubated with100m L GOx (0.1mg mL /C0 1 ) for15minutes at37 1C. On each occasion100m L of H-MnO2NPs (0.1mg mL /C0 1 ) was added. After15minutes, 100m L of TMB solution (1mM) was added gradually, and finally100m L of HAC-NaAC buffer solution (pH = 4.0) over90seconds. Absorbance values were recorded for each trial by using a multi-label microplate reader (Synergy H1, Bio Tek). Some interferences including Na + , fructose, lactose, and maltose were used to replace glucose for specificity experiments. # Results and discussion # Characterization of H-MnO2NPs The H-MnO2NPs prepared via the direct reduction of KMnO4by using citric acid as a reducing agent at room temperature (Fig. 2A) whose morphology was investigated by using SEM and TEM imaging. The H-MnO2NPs are clearly shown to have the expected hollow feature with a diameter of150nm (Fig. 2B) and are assembled by abundant ultrathin MnO2nanosheets Fig. 2 (A) Schematic diagram of the preparation of hollow MnO2NPs, (B) SEM and (C) TEM image of H-MnO2NPs, (D) the HADDF-STEM image (a) and its corresponding EDS mappings (b to d), (E) XRD pattern and (F) FTIR spectra of H-MnO2NPs, KMnO4and citric acid. (Fig. 2C) with large surface area which can increase mass transfer efficiency and enhance catalytic properties. Furthermore, the HAADF-STEM image (Fig. 2D(a)) and the corresponding mappings (Fig. 2D(b-d)) indicated that Mn and O atoms are uniformly distributed on the H-MnO2NPs. The H-MnO2NPs were further characterized by XRD, FTIR and XPS analysis. As shown in Fig. 2E, the diffraction peaks of MnO2NPs fitted well with the standard PDF card (12-0714), indicating the successful preparation of MnO2nanomaterials. The FTIR spectra could be used to investigate the functional groups in H-MnO2NPs and the peaks at550cm /C0 1displayed in Fig. 2F can be attributed to Mn-O bonding, consistent with reports in previous studies. 27,28The peaks at3400cm /C0 1and1650cm /C0 1are characteristic features of -OH and C Q O and can be attributed to citric acid molecules inserted on the surface of the MnO2NPs. Also, the z -potential of H-MnO2NPs was found to be /C0 31.3mV, which is more negative than /C0 30mV, demonstrating that the H-MnO2NPs possess good dispersion stability. 29Thus, the inserted citric acid molecules with rich carboxylic groups appear to increase the dispersion stability of H-MnO2NPs further enhancing the catalytic properties. The elementary compositions of MnO2NPs were determined from the XPS spectra and confirmed the presence of elements C, O and Mn (Fig. S1A, ESI † ). The high-resolution C1s XPS spectra contain three peaks at288.1eV, 285.8eV, and284.0eV which are assigned to C Q O, C-O and C-C, respectively, and which provide further evidence of the presence of residual citric acid molecules on the surface of MnO2NPs (Fig. S1B, ESI † ), consistent with the FTIR analysis. The two peaks at binding energies of642.6eV and654.1eV in the highresolution Mn2p XPS spectra (Fig. S1C, ESI † ) and with a spinenergy separation of11.8eV can be attributed to the Mn2p3/2and Mn2p1/2orbits of Mn4+ . 30 # Intrinsic oxidase activity of H-MnO2NPs The oxidase activity of H-MnO2NPs was evaluated by studying their typical chromogenic reactions using TMB as the substrate. As shown in Fig. 3A, the H-MnO2NPs can oxidize TMB to produce Fig. 3 (A) UV-vis spectra of H-MnO2NPs and Mn2+ in TMB solution, (B) absorbance values of H-MnO2NPs in TMB solution with different incubation times, (C) optimized pH effects of H-MnO2NPs in TMB solution, and (D) absorbance values of H-MnO2NPs, Cu2+ , and Fe2+ with different concentrations in TMB solution. the obvious color change at652nm, whereas Mn2+ does not oxidize TMB (blue line, Fig. 3A), and the possible interference of Mn2+ in the measurements can be excluded. More importantly, H2O2was not necessary for oxidizing TMB, indicating that the H-MnO2NP-TMB system is practical and stable for colorimetric sensing, since any H2O2present is unstable and easily decomposed in air. 31To obtain the best catalytic activity, pH and incubation time were optimized respectively. The absorbance values of the H-MnO2NP-TMB system became stable within90seconds (Fig. 3B) and as can be seen in Fig. 3C, a pH value of4.0was the optimum value to occur. When H-MnO2NP concentration increased, the absorbance values gradually increases which is a desirable attribute for the development of colorimetric biosensing (Fig. 3D). In addition, the H-MnO2NPs exhibited higher catalytic properties than Fe2+ and Cu2+ , indicating that they likely have greater sensitivity for measuring glucose concentration. Furthermore, the kinetic parameters of H-MnO2NPs were obtained based on the Michaelis-Menten equation (Fig. S2A, ESI † ). The Michaelis-Menten constant ( Km) and maximum velocity ( v max) were calculated based on the doublereciprocal plot. Compared with other nanozymes, the proposed H-MnO2NPs possessed smaller Km and higher v max, indicating their excellent oxidase-like activity (Table S1, ESI † ), which could improve the sensitivity of the H-MnO2NP-based sensing system. In addition, the relative activity of the H-MnO2NPs could remain basically unchanged, even though the H-MnO2NPs had been stored for nearly a year (Fig. S3, ESI † ), which demonstrated that the H-MnO2NPs possess good stability. # Colorimetric biosensing of glucose Based on the above-mentioned properties, GOx can oxidize glucose to produce gluconic acid and H2O2, and under acid conditions, H2O2can reduce H-MnO2NPs to generate Mn2+ . The residual MnO2NPs oxidize TMB and produce the colorimetric signals at652nm (Fig. 4A). As expected, when GOx and glucose exist together, the colorimetric signal is decreased, but either GOx or glucose presented individually in the MnO2-TMB system will not cause the colorimetric signal to be decreased (Fig. 4B). Consequently, the difference of colorimetric signals from GOx and glucose was used to quantify the concentrations of glucose. As can be seen in Fig. 4C, when the concentration of glucose is increased from1to200m M, the colorimetric signal gradually decreases and the biosensing colour showed the corresponding intensity of blue with the variable concentration of glucose. Accordingly, a calibration curve could be established relating glucose concentration to the difference of the colorimetric signals and was found to have the following linear relationship y = 0.003x + 0.041, together with a limit of detection of0.84m M (Fig. 4D). The new method has comparable sensitivity with previous literature (Table S2, ESI † ). The enhanced sensitivity can be attributed to the excellent intrinsic oxidase activity of H-MnO2NPs in respect of their hollow structure and large surface area, which can accelerate the reaction kinetics. In addition, the specificity of the new method was demonstrated in that Na + , fructose, lactose, and maltose with10-fold concentration that of glucose were detected by our Fig. 4 (A) Schematic diagram of cascade detection of glucose, (B) UV-vis spectra of GOx, glucose, and GOx + glucose in MnO2-TMB solution, respectively, (C) UV-vis spectra of GOx with different concentrations of glucose in the H-MnO2NP-TMB system, and the picture (inset) of the images of the detection of variable glucose. (D) Calibration curve of glucose detection and (E) specificity of glucose detection. method, but only glucose exhibited an obvious decrease, indicating that the new method possesses high specificity (Fig. 4E). # Colorimetric biosensing of glucose in human serum samples To test the practical feasibility of the new colorimetric method, an exploratory study was performed to measure glucose concentration in samples of human serum obtained from7diabetes patients and7control cases. The results are presented in Table1and show that there is a significant elevation of glucose

Furthermore, the testing results by our method were fitted well with the glucometer. The recoveries ranged from90% to108% indicating that the new method has good potential for commercial exploitation. # Conclusions In conclusion, a rapid and mild method for the preparation of H-MnO2NPs was developed successfully, which were assembled by abundant ultrathin nanosheets. The hollow feature and exposed rich nanosheets can improve the oxidase-like properties of H-MnO2NPs. Taking into account the advantages of good selectivity of GOx and excellent oxidase-like properties of H-MnO2NPs, a cascade strategy for colorimetric biosensing of glucose was performed, exhibiting satisfactory selectivity and sensitivity, as well as practical feasibility in clinical serum analysis. # Conflicts of interest There are no conflicts to declare. # Acknowledgements We acknowledge the financial support from the National Key R&D Program of China (YS2017YFGH000397), the National Natural Science Foundation of China (81720108021), the Zhongyuan Thousand Talents Plan Project-Basic Research Leader Talent (ZYQR201810117) and the Research Start-up Funding of Henan Provincial People's Hospital.
